KENTUCKY'S LEGISLATION AFFECTING AVIATION
The Commonwealth of Kentucky has not just recently decided to enter into the
field of state - fostered and regulated aviation . It began its program in 1926 , and
has made steady and constant progress until at the present time Kentucky has a
simplified set of laws , rules and regulations which , without unduly burdening
anyone , gives the state its control , the fliers freedom of operation , and the general
public assurance of safety and adequateness , not only of the planes in which they
ride , but of the airports from which the planes operate .
The General Assembly at its regular session in 1926 created the Air Board of
Kentucky - a body corporate with authority to sue and be sued , and composed of the
Governor as ex - officio member , the Adjutant General , and three other members
to be appointed by the Governor . The Board was required to hold at least two
regular meetings each year , on the first Monday of January and July . Special
meetings were authorized to be held at the call of the Governor . The Board was
authorized to provide airports and air routes , together with the necessary equipment
and personnel , to foster the advancement of aviation in Kentucky . It , either alone
or with others , was empowered to condemn property for airport purposes , and to do
such other acts as were necessary for the construction , maintenance and equipment
of airports . Any county or city in which there was an airport was authorized to levy
and collect taxes not to exceed ten cents on each one hundred dollars worth of
taxable property in the city or county for airport purposes .
In 1928 , the General Assembly created in each county in which there was a city
of the first class , a bi - partisan Board of six members to be appointed by the mayor
and county judge , to be known as “ The City and County Air Board ." It had for its
purpose supplying air transportation to cities of the first class , and to counties
embracing these cities . Membership on the Board was for a period of four years
and the members thereof served without compensation . The Board was given
authority to engage such employees as were needed and to prescribe such rules and
regulations as it deemed necessary . It was also authorized to acquire land for
airports and facilities , either in the city or county , and to erect such buildings as
were necessary for the operation of the airports . Title to such property was vested
in the Air Board and was exempt from taxation . Sales of such property were
authorized when approved by the mayor or county judge . To carry out the
authority conferred on the Air Board , the city and county each was authorized
to levy an advalorem tax not exceeding three cents on each one hundred
dollars worth of taxable property , proceeds thereof to be administered by the
Board . In anticipation of this and other revenues , the Air Board was empowered
to borrow money .
At the same session of the General Assembly authority was given to the Board
of Park Commissioners of all cities of the first class to purchase property , either
within the city limits or in the county embracing the cities , for the purpose of
establishing an airport . It was given the necessary power to make such rules and
regulations as were necessary to operate the airport , among which was the right to
forbid its use by unlicensed or student pilots . It was also authorized to police and
control this property as other park property was controlled , and to charge and
collect fees for the right to use the airport . The Board was authorized to lease
the airport to others for operation . All revenues received from the use of the
airport were restricted by the Legislature for use and maintenance of the port .
639379
3

In 1930 , the General Assembly extended the authority of the Air Board so as to
give it the right to promulgate air traffic rules identical with those adopted by the
U. S. Government . The Legislature made it unlawful for aircraft to be operated ,
or for persons to operate aircraft in this state unless they were licensed by the
U. S. Government and registered by the State Air Board . It also made it unlawful
to navigate any aircraft in Kentucky in violation of air traffic rules promulgated
by the State Air Board . The Board was directed to cause all airports in the state to
be inspected to determine the safety and adequateness of the port , and made it
unlawful to operate an air field which was declared unfit by the Board . The Legis-
lature appropriated $5,000 to the Board with the provision that no more than
$2,500 should be used as salaries annually to carry out the provisions of the Act.
The General Assembly extended the right of cities to acquire airports so as to
include cities of the second and third classes . These cities were clothed with
power to equip and maintain airports , either within the city limits or in the counties
embracing the cities , and to acquire such lands and material as were necessary for
airports in the same manner in which park property was acquired , equipped and
maintained . The Board was also authorized to promulgate such regulations , and
enforce same , as were necessary for the safety of these fields , and given the right
to contract with the U. S. Government for its use of these airports . The cities were
restricted , however , to the use of all money derived from the airports for main-
tenance thereof and as they had therefor been restricted .
Further progress was made at this session of the General Assembly inasmuch
as it authorized the State Highway Commission to construct and maintain ingress
roads to airports operated by cities and counties in the state . The Highway Com-
mission was restricted , however , to roads not exceeding one mile in length , and not
less than eighteen feet in width , and only when the project was approved by the
State Air Board .
The Legislature at its regular session in 1932 repealed and reenacted the laws
relative to the Air Board of Kentucky . As reenacted , the Air Board was composed
of the Governor , the Lieutenant Governor , the Adjutant General , and two other
members to be appointed by the Governor . The appointed members were required
to be at least twenty - five years of age , white , male , and to hold office at the pleasure
of the Governor . Although the members of the Board served without compensation ,
when attending meetings they were allowed their expenses incurred , and when on
business of the Board were allowed their expenses and a per diem of five dollars .
The Board was required to hold at least two meetings annually , but was permitted
to hold such other meetings as were called by the Governor . It was required to
provide airports and air routes with necessary equipment so as to best foster
commercial and military aviation in Kentucky . For these purposes the Board was
appropriated $ 4,500 a year for the bi - annum of 1932-1933 and 1933-1934 . It was
made a part of the Military Department of Kentucky . The Adjutant General was
the executive officer of the Board .
The Legislature made it unlawful for any person to operate an aircraft in Ken-
tucky unless both the pilot and the aircraft were licensed by the U. S. Government .
The Board was authorized to make rules and regulations applicable to the opera-
tion of aircraft but was required to make them as nearly identical to the rules and
regulations of the U. S. Government as possible . All pilots and aircraft were
required to be registered with the Board , and a period of thirty days was given to
permit such registration . It was made unlawful for any pilot or aircraft to operate
for hire until an application for registration was made to the Board . Registration
was required to be made annually and a registration fee of $ 5 was required to be
paid . The Board was required to inspect all airports in Kentucky to determine
4

their safety and adequateness . It was made unlawful for any person to operate
an aircraft for hire from any airport declared unfit by the Air Board . Any person
tampering with , damaging , or in any way injuring any aircraft , or violating any of
the provisions of the Act, had committed a misdemeanor and upon conviction was
subject to a fine of not more than $ 100 or by imprisonment of not to exceed ninety
days , or subject to both fine and imprisonment .
The General Assembly at its regular 1938 session amended the aviation laws
so as to permit cities of the second , third , fourth , and fifth classes , and counties
containing such cities , to purchase or lease land to establish airports and to sub-
lease airports to other responsible persons for operation .
The Legislature at its regular session in 1940 created the Kentucky Aeronautics
Commission which was to be a bi -partisan group of six members , each of whom
was required to be at least twenty -five years of age , white , and at least two of the
members were required to be affiliated with aviation . This was the first step
toward a comprehensive state aviation program . It is the basis upon which our
present laws are predicated . The Commission was authorized to employ such
agents and employees , including a director , as it deemed necessary . It was also
authorized to prescribe such rules and regulations as it deemed necessary . It con-
tinued in force a requirement that both the pilot and plane must be licensed and
registered with the Civil Aeronautics Authority , and although the Commission
could not require that either the pilot or aircraft be licensed by it , it could without
charging a fee therefor require both the aircraft and pilot to register with the
Commission .
The Kentucky Aeronautics Commission was authorized to promulgate air
traffic rules identical , insofar as practical , with the air traffic rules of the CA A,
and such other rules as the Commission deemed necessary not to conflict with the
rules of the CAA . The Commission was required to cause all airports to be
inspected in order to determine their safety and adequateness , and the Legislature
continued the limitation that aircraft may not be operated for hire from any air-
port declared unfit by the director or the Commission . The members of the Com-
mission and the director were made peace officers and empowered to arrest any
person found violating any provision of the law relating to aviation . The Commis-
sion was authorized to acquire airports and facilities suitable for airport purposes
to operate such airports and provide the necessary personnel , insurance and services
necessary . It was authorized to lease the airports and facilities and to use the
proceeds therefrom to carry out the advancement of aviation . It was authorized to
render engineering and technical assistance to any city in its efforts to acquire an
airport . The Commission was constituted the agency of the state to accept and
receive financial aid from the U. S. Government for the acquisition , construction ,
maintenance and operation of airports , landing fields and air markings .
The General Assembly at its regular 1942 session amended the existing avia-
tion laws so as to allow cities of the sixth class , and the counties embracing the
cities , as well as all other classes of cities , to acquire land for airport purposes .
They were authorized to operate the airports themselves , to lease the airports to
others for operation , or to appoint an Airport Board for this purpose . The Airport
Board so provided for was constituted a body , politic and corporate . It was to be
composed of six members who were citizens , tax payers and regular voters of the
county and were to serve for a period of three years . The Airport Board was
empowered to adopt such regulations , not inconsistent with the legislative body
of the city and county creating the Board , as it deemed necessary . The General
Assembly authorized the refunding of the state excise tax paid on aviation gasoline
used in the transportation of persons and property in interstate commerce on
regular scheduled flights .
5

The General Assembly at its regular 1944 session authorized the Kentucky
Aeronautics Commission to supervise , control and regulate intrastate transportation
of freight and passengers by aircraft for hire . It provided that before any person
shall engage in the public transportation by aircraft of persons or property in
intrastate commerce , it must first obtain a Certificate of Convenience and Necessity
from the Commission , except those persons operating on the basis of their prior
operations shall be granted a Certificate under the grandfather clause of the Act ,
which Certificate shall be effective from the effective date of the Act . Applica-
tions for a Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity were required to be
made in writing to the Commission accompanied by the filing fee of $25 , proof of
insurance , and such other things as required by the Commission . The Commission
was authorized to make enough charges annually for the use of the air routes in
Kentucky as necessary to pay the expenses of the Commission for services rendered
in carrying out their duties in regulating air traffic . However , these charges were
limited so as not to exceed two per cent of the gross receipts of common carriers
operating under the Act . Before the Commission was authorized to issue a Certi-
ficate of Convenience and Necessity , it was required to hold a hearing to determine
the public need and necessity for the routes applied for . All interested persons
were required to be given thirty days notice of the hearing so they could participate
therein . The Commission was required to set up and establish rules and regula-
tions governing the conduct and procedure of such hearings .
The Legislature provided that the applicant or holder of a Certificate might
appeal to the Circuit Court of Franklin County from orders of the Commission ,
and the further right to appeal from the Franklin Circuit Court to the Court of
Appeals of Kentucky . All common carriers subject to the Act were required to
file with the Commission , and keep open for inspection at their offices and terminals ,
schedules showing all rates , fares and charges for the transportation of passengers
and property . The Commission was invested with authority to supervise , regulate
and fix , alter and determine just , fair, reasonable and sufficient rates , fares , charges
and classifications . The Legislature also gave permission for private owners to
apply to the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission to come within its jurisdiction . If
upon inspection of the airport , it was found to conform to the standards of public
airports , the application was required to be approved and the airport was deemed
to be established and within the jurisdiction of the Commission , the same as public
airports . After such an airport had been approved the owner then was permitted
to invoke the aid of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission in condemning property
adjoining the airport needed for airport purposes . By submitting to the jurisdiction
of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission , the private airport owner was then
permitted to extend and improve any runways of the airport over and across any
county road or pass - way not designated by the Kentucky State Highway Depart-
ment as a part of the primary road system of this Commonwealth . The Legislature
also authorized the Department of Highway to furnish men , machinery , material ,
and equipment for the maintenance of runways only of any and all airports estab-
lished by cities , counties , or cities and counties jointly , that have paved runways
and which were approved by the C A A , except if the airport was under lease to the
Federal Government or to a private corporation , in which event the Highway
Department was then without authority to render this assistance .
The authority of each county containing a city of the first class was increased
so as to constitute the City and County Air Board , a body politic and corporate .
Its authority was extended to permit the Air Board to provide as many airports
as were needed to care for the transportation of passengers , property , express and
mail , and for the use of the Federal Government .
At the extraordinary session of the General Assembly which began on April
23 , 1945 , legislative authority was granted to any city or county, or city and county
6

acting together , to issue revenue bonds to defray the cost of purchasing lands ,
buildings , equipment, runways , and other improvements and appurtenances neces-
sary or proper for the establishment and maintenance of airports .
At the regular 1946 session of the General Assembly , there were several major
changes made in the aviation laws . The restriction that all members of the Com-
mission must be white persons was removed . Instead of the members of the Com-
mission serving without compensation , they are allowed $ 10 for each day they are in
attendance at the meetings or hearings of the Commission . Instead of the whole
Commission , or a majority of the members being required to hold hearings or make
investigations , any one member of the Commission was authorized to hold hearings
or make investigations . Any member of the Commission was authorized to issue
subpoenas for witnesses , administer oaths and examine any records pertaining to a
proceeding being held by the Commission .
The Commission members and their employees were specifically authorized to
use their own aircraft , rent or lease aircraft in the discharge of their duties , and
permitted to be reimbursed for the usual and reasonable rental of such aircraft . The
Commission was charged with the duty of at least annually preparing and publishing
a state airport plan for the purpose of coordinating the state and national system of
airports . It was authorized to participate on behalf of any municipality or citizen in
any controversy having to do with any claimed encroachment by the Federal Govern-
ment or any foreign state upon any state or individual rights pertaining to aero-
nautics . The Commission was authorized to enforce the provisions of the Kentucky
aviation laws in addition to any other remedy available , by instituting and prosecut-
ing injunctive proceedings without having to execute a bond . It was granted
authority to acquire , by the same method it was authorized to acquire property for
airport purposes , easements through or other interests in air space as are necessary
to insure safe approaches to the landing areas of airports .
The Legislature declared that the functions of the state and municipalities in
the field of aviation are governmental in character so as to put at rest any doubt
as to the authority of a city or county to appropriate public money for aviation
purposes .
The Legislature also authorized counties in Kentucky that border on other
states and counties contiguous to such bordering counties to acquire , equip , con-
struct , lease , operate , regulate and police airports either within or without the
geographical limit of any such city or county or of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky . The Legislature also authorized political subdivisions to enact and enforce
airport zoning regulations . By this authority any city or county may adopt reason-
able regulations limiting the height to which structures may be erected in a zoning
area around an airport . Instead of authorizing only the refunding of tax paid on
gasoline used in interstate transportation of persons and property , the General
Assembly authorized the refunding of this tax paid on all gasoline designed for and
used in aircraft motors . This enabled not only the airlines operating in interstate
commerce to receive this refund , but authorized the refund to be made to private
flyers as well , whether they operated interstate or intrastate .
This treatise on the legislative program authorized by the General Assembly
has merely touched the high points . Its purpose only is to acquaint the reader of
the trend of legislation in airport development and control in Kentucky .

CHAPTER 183
AVIATION
GENERALLY
183.010 ( 165-48 ) Definitions . ( 1 ) As used in KRS 183.010 to 183.740 , unless
the context otherwise requires :
( a ) " Aircraft " means any contrivance now known , or hereafter invented , used
or designated for navigation of or flight in the air .
( b ) “Public aircraft " means an aircraft used exclusively in the service of any
government or of any political subdivision thereof , including the government of
any state , territory , or possession of the United States , or the District of Columbia ,
but not including any government - owned aircraft engaged in carrying persons or
property for commercial purposes .
( c ) “ Civil aircraft " means any aircraft other than a public aircraft .
( d ) "Airport " means any area , of land or water , which is designed for the
landing and take - off of aircraft , whether or not facilities are provided for the
shelter , servicing , or repair of aircraft , or for receiving or discharging passengers
or cargo , and all appurtenant areas used or suitable for airport building or other
airport facilities , and all appurtenant rights of way , whether heretofore or herein-
after established , except the word " airport , " as the word is used in KRS 183.430
( 183.480 ? ) to 183.510 , shall be synonymous with " landing field , ” or “ aviation field , "
or " landing strip ," and shall indicate and mean any place constructed for the land-
ing or dispatch of machines or aircrafts in the navigation of the air , and which has
been approved and designated for such purposes by the Kentucky Aeronautics
Commission .
( e) "Airport board " or " board " ( except in KRS 183.140 to 183.220 ) means a
board appointed as provided in KRS 183.390 , but this board may be known by
whatever title the city or county , or city and county , may designate .
(f) "Airport hazard ” means any structure , object of natural growth , or use
of land , which obstructs the air space required for the flight of aircraft in landing
or taking off at any airport or is otherwise hazardous to such landing or taking off .
(g ) "Airport facilities " includes land , buildings , equipment , runways and
other improvements and appurtenances necessary or proper for the establishment
and maintenance of airports .
( h ) “Commission " means Kentucky Aeronautics Commission .
( i ) " Operation of aircraft ” or “ operate aircraft " means the use of aircraft for
the purpose of air navigation , and includes the navigation of piloting of aircraft .
Any person who causes or authorizes the operation of aircraft , whether with or
without the right of legal control ( in the capacity of owner , lessee , or otherwise ) of
the aircraft , shall be deemed to be engaged in the operation of aircraft within the
meaning of the statutes of this state .
(j ) " Air navigation " means the operation or navigation of aircraft in the air
space over this state , or upon any airport within this state .
(k) " Navigable air space " means air space above the minimum altitudes of
flight prescribed by the laws of this state or by regulations of the commission con-
sistent therewith .
10

( 1) The term “ common carrier " shall include all carriers for hire or compen-
sation by air who operate , or seek to operate , over fixed routes or between fixed
termini within the State of Kentucky .
(m) The term " charter operator " shall include all persons not included in the
definition of the term " common carrier , " and specifically those persons who trans-
port persons or property in intrastate commerce for hire on unscheduled service and
not between fixed points .
( n ) " Person " means any individual , firm , partnershp , corporation , company ,
association , joint stock association , or body politic ; and includes any trustee , re-
ceiver , assignee , or other similar representative thereof .
( o ) The term " Civil Aeronautics Authority " shall mean that authority or
board of the United States Government empowered to regulate interstate com-
merce by aircraft .
( p ) " Aeronautics " means transportation by aircraft ; the operation , construc-
tion, repair , or maintenance of aircraft , aircraft power plants and accessories , in-
cluding the repair , packing , and maintenance of parachutes ; the design , establish-
ment , construction , extension , operation , improvement , repair , or maintenance of
airports or other air navigation facilities ; and air instruction .
( q ) "Air navigation facility " means any facility other than one owned or con-
trolled by the Federal Government , used in , available for use in , or designed for
use in , aid of air navigation , including airports and any structures , mechanisms ,
lights , beacons , marks , communicating systems , or other instrumentalities or de-
vices used or useful as an aid , or constituting an advantage or convenience , to the
safe taking - off , navigation , and landing of aircraft , or the safe and efficient opera-
tion or maintenance of an airport , and any combination of any or all of such
facilities .
(r ) " City " ( except in KRS 183.140 to 183.220 ) means any city of the second ,
third , fourth , fifth , or sixth class , and " city and county " means a city and county
jointly acquiring an airport under KRS 183.370 .
( s ) “ Governmental unit " means any city , or the combination of any two or
more thereof , or any county , or combination of two or more such counties , city or
cities , acting jointly with any county or counties .
( 2 ) Where a city and county are acting jointly any action contemplated by
KRS 183.630 to 183.740 to be taken by the legislative body of the governmental
unit shall be taken by an ordinance of the city legislative body and parallel or
companion resolution of the fiscal court . ( 1942 , с . 10 , 1; 1944 , с. 24 , 1 ; 1944 , с. 147 , 1 ;
1945 , ex . s ., c . 3 , 1 ; 1946 , c . 48 , § 1 )
NOTE : 1946 , с . 48 , 1, purported to amend only Section 183.010 of the 1944
Edition of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , which would not include subsection ( 5 )
of Section 183.010 as added by 1945 , ex . s ., c . 3. However , the definitions contained
in the 1944 amendment include those set forth in the 1945 amendment, so the 1946
amendment has been treated as superseding the 1945 amendment .
183.020 ( 165-45 ) Kentucky Aeronautics Commission ; Members ; Qualifica-
tions ; Terms .*** ( 1 ) There shall be a Kentucky Aeronautics Commission . The
commission shall be composed of the Governor and six members appointed by the
Governor . The appointive members shall be selected equally from the two political
parties casting the highest and next highest number of votes at the last preceding
general election .
( 2 ) The terms of office of the original appointive members shall expire as:
designated by the Governor at the time of the appointment , two at the end of the
first year , two at the end of the second year , and two at the end of the third year .
11

The two members appointed at the same time shall be members of different politi-
cal parties .
( 3 ) The terms of office of the successors of the original appointees shall be
four years and until their successors are appointed and qualified . Appointments to
fill vacancies shall be for the unexpired term .
(4) All members of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission shall be at least
twenty - five years of age , and at least two members , without regard to their political
affiliations , shall be identified with aviation . ( Subsection ( 4 ) amended , 1946 , с.
48 , § 2 )
183.030 ( 165-45 ; 165-46 ) Compensation ; Officers ; Employes ; Rules and Regu-
lations ; General Powers . ( 1 ) Each member of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commis-
sion shall receive as compensation for actual attendance on regularly assembled
meetings or hearings of the commission ten dollars for each day he attends as shown
by the record and shall be reimbursed for necessary expenses while in attendance .
The commission shall , upon the appointment of its members , organize and elect
officers . It shall elect a chairman , vice - chairman , and secretary from among its
members to serve for one year , and annually thereafter shall elect such officers .
( 2 ) The commission may employ necessary agents and employes , and may pre-
scribe such rules and regulations , other than air traffic rules as provided in KRS
183.080 , as it deems necessary .
( 3 ) The commission shall constitute a corporation with the usual corporate
powers , including the power to acquire and sell real and personal property by pur-
chase , gift , condemnation or otherwise , and may do all things reasonable or neces-
sary to carry out the work contemplated in KRS 183.020 to 183.130 . (1946 , с . 48 , § 3 )
183.031 Investigations , hearings , inquiries and decisions by commission ; orders .
Any investigation , inquiry , or hearing which the commission is authorized to hold
or undertake may be held or undertaken by , or before , any one member of the
commission . Any investigation , inquiry , hearing or decision of the commission ,
and any order made by a member thereof , when approved by a majority of the
members and so shown on a record of its proceedings , shall be considered an order
of the commission . ( 1946 , c . 48 , §8 )
183.032 Process and proceedings ; oaths ; witnesses ; subpoenas ; witness fees ;
compelling production of evidence . ( 1 ) Processes and proceedings under KRS
183.010 to 183.740 shall be as summary and simple as reasonably possible . The
Kentucky Aeronautics Commission or any member thereof , for the purpose of
KRS 183.010 to 183.740 , may subpoena witnesses ; administer , or cause to have
administered , oaths ; and examine , or cause to have examined , such parts of the
books and records of the parties to a proceeding as relate to the question under
consideration .
(2) The sheriff shall serve all subpoenas of the Kentucky Aeronautics Com-
mission and shall receive from the commission the same fee as provided by law
for like service in civil actions .
( 3 ) Each witness who appears in obedience to such subpoena shall receive for
attendance the fees and mileage for witnesses in civil cases in the circuit court to be
paid by the party at whose request the witness was subpoenaed .
(4 ) The circuit court shall , on application of the Kentucky Aeronautics Com-
mission or any member thereof , enforce by proper proceedings the attendance and
testimony of witnesses and the production and examination of books , papers , and
records .
12

183.033 Traveling expenses of Commission and employes ; use of aircraft for
travel . ( 1) The commission and its employes or authorized representatives shall ,
for traveling necessitated by the discharge of official duties , be reimbursed for any
mode of transportation actually paid for , not exceeding the regular fare over the
most direct route , meals and lodging and other necessary expenses actually paid for .
(2) The commission members , their agents and employes , in and for the dis-
charge of the official duties of the commission , are hereby authorized to use their
own aircraft , to contract for hire , rent or lease aircrafts , and be reimbursed for the
usual and reasonable rental on such aircrafts . ( 1946 , c . 48 , §11 )
183.040 ( 165-47 ) ( KENTUCKY AERONAUTICS DIRECTOR ; ) Appointment ;
duties ; powers . The commission shall appoint a Kentucky Aeronautics Director
to hold office during the pleasure of the commission . The director shall hold a
valid commercial license or its equivalent issued by the United States Government
for the piloting of aircraft , and shall have at least five years ' experience in aeronau-
tics . The director shall be the executive officer of the commission , and under its
supervision , shall administer and carry out the provisions of this chapter , and the
rules and regulations and orders of the commission established thereunder as well
as the laws of the United States and the State of Kentucky with reference to aero-
nautics . He shall be in charge of the office of the commission and be responsible
to the commission for the preparation of reports and the collection and dissemination
of public information relating to aviation . At the direction of the commission the
director shall , together with the chairman of the commission , execute all contracts
entered into by the commission . The director may attend , but not vote , at all
meetings of the commission . ( 1946 , c . 48 , § 5 )
183.050 ( 165-49 ) Licensing and registering aircraft . No person shall navigate
an aircraft unless it is licensed and registered by the Civil Aeronautics Authority
or other proper department of the United States in the manner prescribed by the
lawful rules and regulations of the United States then in force .
183.060 ( 165-50 ) Licensing of pilots ; qualifications . No person shall navigate
an aircraft unless he is licensed and registered by the Civil Aeronautics Authority
or other proper department of the United States in the manner described by the
lawful rules and regulations of the United States then in force .
183.070 ( 165-51 ) No license to be prescribed by Kentucky Aeronautics Com-
mission ; registration of pilots and aircraft . No license other than that prescribed
by the Civil Aeronautics Authority or other proper department of the United States
for aircraft or pilots shall be required within this state . The commission may re-
quire that all aircraft , or pilots operating aircraft frequently or regularly from any
airport within this state , shall register with the commission , but no fee shall be
imposed for such registration .
183.080 ( 165-52 ) Air traffic rules to be identical with federal rules . The
commission may promulgate air traffic rules applicable to the operation of aircraft
within the state , but the rules shall be identical with the air traffic rules adopted
by the Civil Aeronautics Authority or other proper department of the United States
as nearly as possible and in so far as applicable . The air traffic rules promulgated
by the commission shall be maintained in current condition by promulgating any
amendments which are made effective by the Civil Aeronautics Authority or other
proper department of the United States . The commission may also promulgate
local rules , not in conflict with the Civil Aeronautics Authority rulings , necessary
for safety .
Administrative regulations not effective until filed with Secretary of State ,
KRS 13.020 .
13

183.090 ( 165-54 ) Inspection of airports ; unlawful to operate from airport de-
clared unfit ; issuance and revocation of airport licenses or certificates of approval .
( 1 ) The commission shall , by the Director of Aeronautics or otherwise , cause all
airports in the state to be inspected to determine the safety and adequateness of
the facilities for the operation of aircraft . No person shall operate any aircraft for
hire from an airport declared unfit for operation by the director or the commission ,
until the airport is approved by the commission .
( 2 ) In determining whether it shall issue a certificate of approval or license
for the use or operation of any proposed airport , the commission shall take into
consideration its proposed location , size and layout , the relationship of the proposed
airport to a comprehensive plan for state - wide and nation - wide development ,
whether there are safe areas available for expansion purposes , whether the adjoin-
ing area is free from obstructions based on a proper glide ratio , the nature of the
terrain , the nature of the uses to which the proposed airport will be put , and the
possibilities for future development.
( 3 ) The commission is empowered to temporarily or permanently revoke any
certificate of approval or license issued by it when it shall determine that an air-
port or other navigation facility is not being maintained or used in accordance with
the provisions of KRS 183.010 to 183.740 and the rules and regulations lawfully
promulgated pursuant thereto . ( 1946 , c . 48 , § 6 )
183.100 ( 165-53 ) Unlawful to operate in violation of air traffic rules . No
person shall operate any aircraft within this state in any form of navigation whatso-
ever in violation of the air traffic rules promulgated by the commission .
183.110 ( 165-55 ) Members of commission and director are peace officers . Each
member of the commission and the Director of Aeronautics shall be peace officers
and may arrest any person found violating any provision of KRS 183.020 to 183.130
with respect to operation of aircraft .
183.120 ( 165-57 ) Power of commission to provide airports and facilities . ( 1)
The commission may acquire or sell , by contract , purchase , lease , donation or con-
demnation , airports , buildings , runways , grounds , and other facilities suitable for
airport purposes and the proper safeguards to flying where such airports , etc. , shall
reasonably inure to the benefit of the state .
(2 ) The commission may make additions and improvements to such airports
or facilities and either alone or with the cooperation of others provide personnel ,
heat , light , water , fuel , telephone service , drainage , runways , fueling facilities , and
other costs of operation and maintenance , including insurance , and may bear the
expense of removal or change of obstructions that menace air travel .
( 3 ) Title to all property acquired or constructed by the commission shall vest
in the commission so long as it is held for the purpose of establishing and maintain-
ing airports and such other purposes as are naturally incident thereto , to provide
for transportation by air of passengers , freight , express or mail matter , or whatever
else the public generally may require to be carried .
( 4 ) The commission may enter into contracts of lease for land or facilities to
which title is vested in the commission with any city , political subdivision or person
for the furtherance of the purposes of KRS 183.020 to 183.130 . All rents or revenues
derived from such contracts of lease shall become the property of the commission
to be expended by it in carrying out the purposes of KRS 183.020 to 183.130 .
( 5 ) The commission may give such advice and assistance , including financial
aid and engineering and technical assistance within the limits of its resources as it
deems advisable , to enable any city or political subdivision to acquire , construct ,
14

maintain and operate airports or otherwise assist in the development of aeronautics
within their limits .
183.121 State airways systems ; state airport plan ; participation of commission
in controversies involving aeronautics ; injunctive proceedings . ( 1 ) The Kentucky
Aeronautics Commission may designate , design , and establish , expand , or modify a
state airways system which will best serve the interests of the state . It may chart
such airways system and arrange for publication and distribution of such maps and
charts and notices and bulletins relating to such airways as may be required in the
public interest . The system shall be supplementary to and coordinated in design
and operation with the federal airways system . It may include all types of air
navigation facilities , whether publicly or privately owned , provided that such
facilities conform to federal safety standards .
(2 ) The commission shall annually , or as often as it deems necessary , prepare
and publish a State Airport Plan for the purpose of coordinating and effecting a
state and national system of airports . The commission shall , in the preparation of
said plan , take into consideration the local needs for an airport , the state and
national system of airports , and any program inaugurated by the Federal Govern-
ment to render aid for the development of airports .
(3 ) It may participate as party plaintiff or defendant , or as intervener on be-
half of the state or any municipality or citizen thereof , in any controversy having
to do with any claimed encroachment by the Federal Government or any foreign
state upon any state or individual rights pertaining to aeronautics .
(4 ) To enforce the provisons of KRS 183.010 to 183.740 , the commission may in
addition to all other remedies institute and prosecute injunctive proceedings without
the execution of a bond . ( 1946 , c . 48 , § 4)
183.122 Acquisition of airport protection privileges , and right to place and
maintain hazard markers and lights . Where necessary , in order to provide unob-
structed air space for the landing and taking off of aircraft utilizing airports ac-
quired or operated under the provisions of KRS 183.010 to 183.740 , the Kentucky
Aeronautics Commission is hereby granted authority to acquire , in the same manner
as is provided for the acquisition of property for airport purposes , easements through
or other interests in air space over land or water , interests in airport hazards outside
the boundaries of the airports and such other airport protection privileges as are
necessary to insure safe approaches to the landing areas of said airports , and the
safe and efficient operation thereof . The Kentucky Aeronautics Commission is also
hereby authorized to acquire , in the same manner , the right or easement , for a term
of years or perpetually , to place or maintain suitable marks for the daytime marking
and suitable lights for the nighttime marking of airport hazards , including the right
of ingress and egress to or from such airport hazards for the purpose of maintaining
and repairing such lights and marks . This authority shall not be so construed as to
limit the right , power , or authority of the state or any municipality to zone property
adjacent to any airport pursuant to any law of this state . ( 1946 , c . 48 , § 7 )
183.123 Functions by State in field of aviation are governmental in character .
The acquisition of any lands for the purpose of establishing airports or other air
navigation facilities ; the acquisition of any airport protection privileges ; the acqui-
sition , establishment , construction , enlargement , improvement , maintenance , equip-
ment , and operation of airports and other air navigation facilities , whether by the
state separately or jointly with any governmental unit thereof ; the assistance of this
state in any such acquisition , establishment , construction , enlargement , improve-
ment, maintenance , equipment , and operation ; and the exercise of any other powers
granted to the commission in KRS 183.010 to 183.740 are hereby declared to be public
and governmental functions , exercised for a public purpose , and matters of public
15

necessity , and such lands and other property and privileges acquired and used by
the state in the manner and for the purposes enumerated in KRS 183.010 to 183.740
shall and are hereby declared to be acquired and used for public and governmental
purposes and as a matter of public necessity . ( 1946 , с. 48 , § 12 )
183.130 ( 165-58 ) Federal aid ; commission agency to receive . ( 1 ) The Com-
mission is the proper agency of the state to accept and receive financial or other aid
from the United States Government for the acquisition , construction , maintenance
and operation of airports , air markings and other air navigational aids or otherwise
in carrying out the promotion of aviation , whether the work is to be done by the
state or at the expense of cities or political subdivisions aided by grants or aids from
the United States .
( 2 ) When acquisition or construction of airports or other facilities is made at
the joint expense of cities or political subdivisions and the United States Govern-
ment , the commission shall , on behalf of such cities or political subdivisions , accept
and receipt for any money paid over by the United States Government and forth-
with pay such money over to the cities or political subdivisions under the terms
and conditions imposed by the United States Government in making the grants .
Disposition of private funds and contributions available to state departments and
agencies , KRS 41.290 .
183.140 ( 938j - 1 ) Air boards in counties having first - cass cities . ( 1 ) In each
county in which there is a city of the first class there shall be a bi - partisan board
composed of six members , known as the ( name of city ) and ( name of county ) Air
Board . The board shall be a body politic and corporate with the usual corporate
attributes , and in its corporate name may sue and be sued , contract and be con-
tracted with , and do all things reasonable or necessary to effectively carry out the
work contemplated by KRS 183.140 to 183.210 . ( 1944 , с . 138 , § 1)
(2 ) The members of the board shall be appointed jointly by the mayor of the
first - class city and the county judge . The members of the board shall be selected
equally from the two political parties casting the highest and next highest number
of votes at the last preceding general election .
( 3 ) Members of the board shall serve for terms of four years each , and until
their successors are appointed and qualified . The members shall be bona fide resi-
dents of the county .
Department of public parks and recreation in first - class cities to control air-
ports formerly controlled by park board , KRS 97.252
183.150 ( 938j - 6 ) Purposes of board ; powers . The purpose of the board shall
be to establish and maintain one or more airports and facilities to provide for trans-
portation by air of passengers , property , express and mail , and to provide one or
more airports and facilities for the use of the Federal Government . The board
shall have such powers as may be necessary or desirable to promote aviation and
the development of facilities for air travel and transportation . ( 1944 , c . 138 , § 2 )
183.160 ( 938j - 2 ) Compensation ; organization ; rules . ( 1 ) Members of the
board shall serve without compensation but shall be allowed any reasonable ex-
penses incurred by them in the conduct of the affairs of the board . The commission
shall , upon the appointment of its members , organize and elect officers . It shall
elect a chairman from its members to serve for one year , and it shall annually elect
thereafter a chairman .
(2 ) The board shall employ necessary agents and employes to carry out its
work and functions , and to prescribe such rules and regulations as it deems
necessary .
Eminent Domain , Ch . 416
16

183.170 ( 938j - 1 ; 939j - 3 ) Board may acquire land and establish airports . The
board may acquire by contract , lease , purchase , gift , condemnation or otherwise any
real or personal property necessary or suitable for establishing airports , and air
navigation facilities in the city or county . The board may erect , equip , operate and
maintain , on such land , buildings and equipment necessary and proper to establish ,
conduct and maintain airports or air navigation facilities .
183.180 ( 938j - 7 ) Condemnation . ( 1) The board may , by a resolution reciting
that property within the city or county is needed for purposes contemplated by KRS
183.150 , direct the condemnation of such property .
(2) Condemnation proceedings shall be conducted in the name of the board
by the city attorney who shall be the local representative of the board .
( 3 ) The procedure for the condemnation shall conform to the procedure set
out in KRS 416.120 .
183.190 ( 938j - 4 ) County and city may levy tax for airport purposes . In order
to provide money for the purchase of lands and other property necessary for the
establishment of airports , and to establish , equip and maintain necessary buildings
for the purposes of airports , the fiscal court of the county and the legislative body
of each city of the first class located in the county in which such airports exist , may
make an annual levy to collect a tax not exceeding three cents on each one hundred
dollars ' worth of taxable property in the county and city respectively . The proceeds
of the tax shall be turned over to the board for the purposes set forth in KRS 183.150 .
183.200 ( 938j - 5 ) Board may borrow money and pledge revenue . The board
may borrow money on its own credit in anticipation of the revenue to be derived
from taxes or other income for the fiscal year in which the money is borrowed , and
for such purposes the board may pledge the taxes , if any , authorized under KRS
183.190 .
183.205 Board may issue revenue bonds . The joint city - county air board of a
city of the first class and of the county in which such city is located is authorized to
defray the cost of acquiring any airport facilities through the issuance of revenue
bonds in the same manner and under the same conditions as are provided in KRS
183.630 to 183.740 are granted to or imposed upon governmental units and the legis-
lative bodies thereof . The provisions of KRS 183.150 to 183.220 shall apply in the
case of airport facilities so acquired . ( 1945 , ex . s ., c . 3 , § 131/2 )
183.210 ( 938j - 6 ) Title to property ; contracts with state and federal govern-
ments ; tax exemption ; alienation of property . ( 1) Title to all property acquired
for the purposes of KRS 183.150 shall vest in the board , and shall be held in trust
for the purposes of KRS 183.150 .
( 2 ) The board may contract with the Federal Government and with any state
government for use by such governments of the airport and facilities .
( 3 ) The property acquired by the board shall be free from all taxation or
assessments . The board may, with the approval of the county judge and mayor , sell ,
exchange , transfer and convey any of the property acquired by it. The proceeds of
the disposition of the property shall be held by the board in trust for the purposes
set out in KRS 183.150 .
Department of public parks and recreation in first - class to control airports
formerly controlled by park board , KRS 97.252 .
183.220 ( 1840d ) Certain counties may appropriate money for airports ; when .
In counties having a population of 150,000 or more , the fiscal court may appropriate
and expend out of the county funds a sum not to exceed $5,000 in any one year for
the construction , repair or maintenance of airports used or to be used by the Fed
17

eral Government in connection with its postal service , or as an airport for aviation
service of the government , or its reserve officers in such service , or by the county
or any city located in the county to provide a public airport .
183.230 to 183.290 ( 2859a - 1 to 2859a - 7 ) Repealed , 1942 , c . 34 , § 5. ( See Note to
KRS 97.250 )
183.300 to 183.360 ( 2741q to 2741q - 5 ) Repealed , 1942 , c . 10 , § 15 .
183.370 Cities of second to sixth class , and counties , may acquire and operate
airports , alone or jointly ; control of existing airports . ( 1 ) Any city of the second ,
third , fourth , fifth or sixth class , or any county or any such city jointly with the
county in which it is located, may acquire , either by lease , purchase , gift, or con-
demnation in the same manner as park property is condemned , any land necessary
or suitable for establishing airports within or without the limits of such city or
county . Such cities and counties may erect , equip , operate and maintain , on the
land , buildings , equipment , runways and other things necessary or proper for the
establishment and maintenance of airports .
(2) All airports already acquired by cities or counties or cities and counties
mentioned in subsection ( 1 ) shall be subject to the provisions of KRS 183.370 to
183.470 , but those sections shall not impair existing contracts as to the acquisition
and use of such airports . ( 1942 , c . 10 , § 2 )
Condemnation for park purposes , KRS 416.120
183.380 Lease of airport ; management with or without airport board . The city
or county or city and county may lease the airport to one or more persons upon such
terms as may be provided by ordinance or resolution , or may place the management
and control of the airport under an airport board , or may operate the airport with-
out the appointment of a board . ( 1942 , c . 10 , § 3 )
183.390 Appointment of airport board ; membership ; qualifications ; term ; in-
compatibility with other office . ( 1 ) The city or county or city and county may by
ordinance or resolution provide for the appointment of an airport board , composed
of six members , to operate , manage and control the airport . The legislative body of
the city or county or city and county shall , by the adoption of such ordinance or
resolution , prescribe the rules and regulations by which the board shall be governed .
( 2 ) The members of the airport board shall be citizens , taxpayers and legal
voters of the county , and no member of the board shall be interested directly or
indirectly in any contract for the furnishing of supplies or of service of any kind to
the airport .
( 3 ) The first airport board appointed shall serve for terms as follows : Two for
one year , two for two years , and two for three years . Upon the expiration of the
first terms , successors shall be appointed for a term of three years . In the case of
an airport jointly owned by a city and county the members shall be appointed so
that the terms of one city member and one county member shall expire each year .
(4 ) If at any time during his term of office any member of the airport board
becomes a candidate for nomination or is elected or appointed to any public office ,
he shall automatically vacate his membership and another person shall be appointed
to serve for the remainder of his term , or until a successor has been appointed .
(1942 , с . 10, § 4 )
183.400 Board is corporation ; bylaws , rules and regulations ; compensation . ( 1 )
The airport board shall be a body - politic and corporate , and shall have the usual
corporate powers .
(2) The airport board may make and adopt its own bylaws , rules and regula-
18

E
tions , not inconsistent with those of the legislative body or bodies creating it , for
the orderly , efficient and economic operation of the airport .
( 3 ) Members of the airport board shall serve without compensation unless by
ordinance or resolution a salary is fixed , in which event the salary of each member
of said board shall not exceed three hundred dollars per year . ( 1942 , c . 10 , § 5, § 6 )
183.410 Officers of board . ( 1 ) The airport board shall choose a member of
the board as president and shall also elect a secretary - treasurer who may or may
not be a member of the board .
( 2 ) The legislative body or bodies shall by ordinance or resolution fix the
salary of the secretary - treasurer at not exceeding five hundred dollars per year .
The secretary -treasurer shall execute an official bond , to be approved by the board
and the cost thereof shall be paid by the legislative body or bodies which created
the board . ( 1942 , c . 10 , § 7 )
183.420 Duties of secretary -treasurer . ( 1 ) The secretary - treasurer shall
keep the minutes of all meetings of the board and shall also keep a set of books
showing the receipts and expenditures of the board . He shall preserve on file
duplicate vouchers for all expenditures . Such books and vouchers shall at all times
be subject to the examination of the legislative body by whom the board is created .
( 2 ) The secretary - treasurer shall annually in January transmit to the legisla-
tive body a detailed report of all acts and doings of the board . The report shall show
a complete accounting of the board's financial obligations for the preceding year , an
itemized account of all receipts and disbursements of money , and an itemized esti-
mate of the money required for operation of the airport during the next year . ( 1942 ,
c . 10, § 8 )
183.430 Regulations as to use of Airport ; charges ; policing . The city or county
or city and county may make rules and regulations for the use and safety of airports
and may deny to anyone not a regular licensed pilot or student under a licensed pilot
the right to use the airport . They may establish such rates , charges or fees for the
use of the airports as they deem best . The airports shall be policed in the manner
determined by the city or county or city and county . ( 1942 , c . 10 , § 9 )
183.440 Contracts for use of airport . The city or county or city and county
may contract with any person or governmental agency for the use of the airport .
Such contract shall not prevent the general use of the airport by the citizens of the
city or county . ( 1942 , c . 10 , § 10 )
183.450 Expenditures for airport . All moneys derived from the use of the air-
port shall be first applied to the maintenance of the airport , and the city or county
or city and county may expend out of their general funds or any other funds avail-
able for the maintenance of the airport . ( 1942 , c . 10 , § 11 )

regulate the transportation of persons and property for hire by aircraft operating in

companies ; conditions of issuance ; present operators ; operators with federal certifi-
cate ; rights granted by certificate . ( 1 ) Any person seeking to engage in the opera-

Aeronautics Board , or any other such Federal agency , before June 13 , 1944. ( 1944 ,

183.550 Form of application for certificate ; fee ; charges for expenses of commis-
sion . Application for such certificate shall be in writing , and shall contain such in-
formation as the commission may require . All applications for a certificate shall be
in the form prescribed by the commission by general rule and shall be accompanied
by a filing fee of twenty - five dollars , proof of insurance and all other things re-
quired by law and the rules and regulations of the commission . The commission
is empowered to make enough charges annually for the use of air routes in the
State of Kentucky to pay the expenes of said commission for services rendered

ceipts from intrastate business of any common carrier operating hereunder , and all

ate as a common carrier or charter operator in intrastate commerce , the commission
shall fix a time and place for a hearing thereon and shall cause notice of the filing of
such application and of the hearing thereon to be given by mail not less than thirty
days , exclusive of the date of mailing , before such hearing , addressed to all holders
of certificates and applicants therefor whose operations , or proposed operations
would be affected by the granting of the proposed certificate , and upon any other
person deemed by the commission to have an interest in such proceeding , and said
notice shall likewise be given to the mayors of all cities on said route . All inter-
ested persons shall have the right to appear and take part in all proceedings before
the commission either in person or by counsel . Hearings on such applications shall

183.570 Determination of application ; rules and regulations ; terms of certifi-
cates ; evidence as to convenience and necessity ; duration of certificates , revocation ,
transfer ; rights conferred by certificate ; mail carriers ; jurisdiction of commission ;
service of orders ; rehearing . ( 1 ) The commission is hereby vested with power and
authority and it is hereby made its duty upon the filing of an application for a
certificate of public convenience and necessity to hear and determine under such
reasonable rules and regulations as it may promulgate whether public convenience
and necessity require the proposed service . No such hearings shall be had until
thirty days after rules and regulations made and promulgated have been published
and filed with the Secretary of State of Kentucky . The commission may issue such
certificate granting the application in whole or in part and may impose such terms
and conditions as it may deem proper in the public interest . The applicant for such
certificate shall be entitled thereto only upon showing by competent evidence that
the public convenience and necessity requires the proposed service or operation . The
commission shall receive and consider evidence with respect to other forms of trans-
portation , or the service , or facilities thereof , in determining whether public con-
venience and necessity requires the proposed service or operation . All certificates
issued by the commission shall continue in force and effect until surrendered or re-
voked for cause . No certificate may be transferred except upon proper application
and hearing and upon a finding by the commission that such transfer is consistent
with the public interest . No certificate shall confer any proprietary , property , or
exclusive rights in the use of any air space , air port , or other air - navigation facility .
(2 ) No certificate shall be required for the transportation of the United States
mail unless passengers or freight are transported . The juridiction of the commission
shall extend only to common carriers and charter operators engaged in , or who seek
to engage in , intrastate commerce . Orders of the commission shall be served upon all
parties to said proceedings by the commission and shall become effective thirty days
after such service . Any of said parties to any such hearing , may upon proper
petition be granted a rehearing . If any rehearing is granted within thirty days after
the issuance of an order , then such order shall not become effective until thirty
days after the determination of the commission following said rehearing proceed-
ings . ( 1944 , c . 147 , § 6 )
183.580 Rate schedules and classifications ; power of commission to regulate
and fix rates . All common carriers subject to KRS 183.530 to 183.620 shall before
engaging in business file with the commission and keep open to public inspection at
its offices and terminals schedules showing all rates , fares and charges for trans-
portation of passengers , or property between different points on its route or routes ,
and also between points on its own route and on the routes of other common carriers ,
when a through route and joint rate have been established . No intrastate common
aircraft carrier shall accept or receive any person or property for transportation
until the requirements of KRS 183.530 to 183.620 have been complied with and no
such carrier shall charge , demand , collect , or receive , a greater or less or different
remuneration for such transportation , or for any service in connection therewith
than the rates , fares and charges which have been legally established and filed with
the commission ; nor shall any such carrier refund or remit in any manner or by any
device , any portion of the rates , fares and charges so established when service is
rendered in accordance with published tariffs . The commission is vested with the
power and authority to supervise , regulate and fix , alter and determine just , fair ,
reasonable and sufficient rates , fares , charges and classifications . No order of the
commission affecting rates , fares and charges shall issue except after notice and
hearing on the same and any interested party may appear and take part in any such
proceedings . ( 1944 , c . 147 , § 7 )

cies ; companies to give bond ; publication of maps . (1 ) The commission is em-

motion , and upon at least thirty days ' notice to the parties affected thereby , and for
good cause and after an opportunity for a hearing on the same , revoke , suspend,
alter , amend or modify any and all certificates of convenience and necessity to

petition shall state completely the grounds upon which the review is sought , and

hearing before said commission .

183.630 Governmental unit may issue revenue bonds for acquisition of airport
facilities . For the purpose of defraying the cost of acquiring any airport facilities ,
either by purchase or construction , a governmental unit may borrow money and
issue negotiable bonds , but only after an ordinance or resolution has been adopted
by the legislative body specifying the proposed undertaking , the amount of bonds to
be issued and the maximum rate of interest the bonds are to bear . The ordinance
or resolution shall further provide that the airport facilities are to be acquired pur-
suant to the provisions of KRS 183.630 to 183.740 . ( 1945 , ex . s ., c . 3 , § 2 )
183.640 Interest rate and maturity of bonds . The bonds may be issued to bear
interest at a rate not to exceed six percent per annum , payable either annually or
semi - annually , and shall be executed in such manner and be payable at such times
not exceeding twenty -five years from date thereof and at such place or places as the
legislative body determines . ( 1945 , ex . s ., c . 3, § 3 )
183.650 Bonds negotiable and tax free ; method of sale ; interest yield ; payable
solely from revenues . Bonds issued pursuant to KRS 183.630 to 183.740 shall be
negotiable and shall not be subject to taxation . If any officer whose signature or
countersignature appears on the bonds or coupons ceases to be such officer before
delivery of the bonds , his signature or countersignature shall nevertheless be valid
and sufficient for all purposes the same as if he had remained in office until de-
livery . The bonds shall be sold in such manner and upon such terms as the legis-
lative body of the governmental unit deems best , or any contract for the acquisition
of any airport facilities may provide that payment shall be made in such bonds . In
no event shall any bonds be negotiated on a basis to yield more than six percent .
The bonds shall be payable solely from the revenue derived from the airport facili-
ties , and shall not constitute an indebtedness of the governmental unit within the
meaning of the Constitution . It shall be plainly stated on the face of each bond that
it has been issued under the provisions of KRS 183.630 to 183.740 and that it does

not constitute an indebtedness of the governmental unit within the meaning of the
Constitution . ( 1945 , ex . s ., c . 3, § 4 )
183.660 Application of proceeds of bonds . All money received from the bonds
shall be applied solely for the acquisition of the airport facilities , and the necessary
expense of preparing , printing and selling said bonds , or to advance the payment of
the interest on the bonds during the first three years following the date of the
bonds . ( 1945 , ex . s ., c . 3 , § 5 )
