AIRPORT ZONING
183.750 Definitions . As used in KRS 183.751 to 183.758 , unless the context
otherwise requires :
( 1) "Airport " means any area , of land or water , which is designed for the
landing and take - off of aircraft , whether or not facilities are provided for the shelter ,
servicing , or repair of aircraft , or for receiving or discharging passengers or cargo ,
and all appurtenant areas used or suitable for airport building or other airport
facilities , and all appurtenant rights of way , whether heretofore or hereafter
established .
(2) "Airport hazard " means any structure , object of natural growth , or use of
land , which obstructs the air space required for the flight of aircraft in landing or
taking off at any airport or is otherwise hazardous to such landing or taking off .
( 3 )
(4 )
" Political subdivision " means any city or county .
" Legislative body , " in the case of county , means the fiscal court .
( 5 ) " Structure " means any object constructed or installed by man, including ,
but without limitation , buildings , towers , smoke - stacks , and overhead transmission
lines .
( 6 ) " Ordinance , " in the case of a county , means resolution of the fiscal court .
(1946 , c . 49 , § 1 )
183.751 Airport hazards are not in public interest . It is hereby found and de-
clared that an airport hazard endangers the lives and property of users of the air-
port and of occupants of land in the vicinity , and also , if of the obstruction type , in
effect reduces the size of the area available for landing , taking - off and maneuvering
of aircraft , thus tending to destroy or impair the utility of the airport and the public
investment therein , and is therefore not in the interest of the public health , public
safety or general welfare . ( 1946 , c. 49 , § 2 )
183.752 Political subdivisions may adopt airport zoning regulations ; what to
embrace ; adoption in connection with general zoning ordinance ; joint zoning ; zon-
27

ing beyond limits of political subdivision ; regulations must be reasonable . ( 1)
Every political subdivision may adopt , administer , and enforce , under the police
power and in the manner and upon the conditions hereinafter prescribed , airport
zoning regulations for any airport within its jurisdiction , which regulations shall
divide the area surrounding the airport into zones , and , within such zones , specify
the land uses permitted , and regulate and restrict the height to which structures and
trees may be erected or allowed to grow . In adopting or revising any such zoning
regulations , the political subdivision shall consider , among other things , the char-
acter of the flying operations expected to be conducted at the airport , the nature of
the terrain , the height of existing structures and trees above the level of the air-
port , the possibility of lowering or removing existing obstructions , and the views
of the agency of the Federal Government charged with the fostering of civil aero-
nautics , as to the Aerial approaches necessary to safe flying operations at the airport .
( 2 ) In the event that a political subdivision has adopted , or hereafter adopts ,
a general zoning ordinance regulating , among other things , the height of buildings ,
any airport zoning regulations adopted for the same area or portion thereof under
KRS 183.750 to 183.758 may be incorporated in and made a part of such general zon-
ing regulations , and be administered and enforced in connection therewith , but
such general zoning regulations shall not limit the effectiveness or scope of the
regulations adopted under KRS 183.750 to 183.758 .
( 3 ) Any two or more political subdivisions may agree , by ordinance duly
adopted , to create a joint board and delegate to said board the powers conferred in
KRS 183.750 to 183.758 to promulgate , administer and enforce airport zoning regula-
tions to protect the aerial approaches of any airport located within the corporate
limits of any one or more of said political subdivisions . Such joint boards shall
have as members two representatives appointed by the chief executive officer of
each political subdivision participating in the creation of said board , and a chairman
elected by a majority of the members so appointed .
(4 ) The jurisdiction of each political subdivision is hereby extended to the
promulgation , administering and enforcement of airport zoning regulations to pro-
tect the approaches of any airport which is owned by said political subdivision but
located wholly or partially outside the corporate limits of said political subdivision .
In case of conflict with any airport zoning or other regulations promulgated by any
other political subdivision , the regulations adopted pursuant to this subsection shall
prevail .
( 5 ) All airport zoning regulations adopted under KRS 183.750 to 183.758 shall
be reasonable , and none shall require the removal , lowering , or other change or
alteration of any structure or tree not conforming to the regulations when adopted
or amended , or otherwise interfere with the continuance of any nonconforming use ,
except as provided in subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 183.753 . ( 1946 , c. 49 , § 3 )
183.753 Granting of permits ; nonconforming uses ; variances ; marking and
lighting of obstructions . ( 1 ) Where advisable to facilitate the enforcement of zoning
regulations adopted pursuant to KRS 183.750 to 183.758 , a system may be established
by any political subdivision for the granting of permits to establish or construct new
structures and other uses and to replace existing structures and other uses or make
substantial changes therein or substantial repairs thereof . In any event , before any
nonconforming structure or tree may be replaced , substantially altered or repaired ,
rebuilt , allowed to grow higher , or replanted , a permit must be secured from the
administrative agency authorized to administer and enforce the regulations , author-
izing such replacement , change or repair . No such permit shall be granted that
would allow the structure or tree in question to be made higher or become a greater
hazard to air navigation than it was when the applicable regulation was adopted ;
and whenever the administrative agency determines that a nonconforming struc
28

ture or tree has been abandoned or more than eighty percent torn down , destroyed ,
deteriorated , or decayed :
( a ) No permit shall be granted that would allow said structure or tree to ex-
ceed the applicable height limit or otherwise deviate from the zoning regulations ;
and
( b ) Whether application is made for a permit under this subsection or not ,
the said agency may by appropriate action compel the owner of the nonconforming
structure or tree , at his own expense , to lower , remove , reconstruct , or equip such
object as may be necessary to conform to the regulations or , if the owner of the
nonconforming structure or tree shall neglect or refuse to comply with such order
for ten days after notice thereof , the said agency may proceed to have the object
so lowered , removed , reconstructed , or equipped and assess the cost and expense
thereof upon the object or the land whereon it is or was located . Unless such an
assessment is paid within ninety days from the service of notice thereof on the
agent or owner of such object or land , the sum shall bear interest at the rate of
ten per cent per annum until paid , and shall be collected in the same manner as are
general taxes .
( 2 ) Except as indicated in subsection ( 1 ) , all applications for permits for re-
placement , change or repair of nonconforming uses shall be granted .
( 3 ) Any person desiring to erect any structure , or increase the height of any
structure , or permit the growth of any tree , or otherwise use his property , in vio-
lation of airport zoning regulations adopted under KRS 183.750 to 183.758 , may
apply to the board of appeals , as provided in KRS 183.755 , for a variance from the
zoning regulations in question . Such variances shall be allowed where a literal
application or enforcement of the regulations would result in practical difficulty
or unnecessary hardship and the relief granted would not be contrary to the public
interest but do substantial justice and be in accordance with the spirit of the regu-
lations and KRS 183.750 to 183.758 .
( 4) In granting any permit or variance under this section , the administrative
agency or board of appeals may, if it deems such action advisable to effectuate the
purposes of KRS 183.750 to 183.758 and reasonable in the circumstances , so condition
such permit or variance as to require the owner of the structure or tree in question
to permit the political subdivision , at its own expense , to install , operate , and main-
tain suitable obstruction markers and obstruction lights thereon . ( 1946 , c . 49 , § 4 )
183.754 Procedure for adoption of zoning regulations ; administration and en-
forcement ; administrative agency . ( 1 ) No airport zoning regulations shall be
adopted , amended , or changed under KRS 183.750 to 183.758 except by action of the
legislative body of the political subdivision in question , or the joint board provided
for in KRS 183.752 , after a public hearing in relation thereto , at which parties of
interest and citizens shall have an opportunity to be heard . At least fifteen days '
notice of the hearing shall be published in an official paper , or a paper of general
circulation , in the political subdivision or subdivisions in which the airport is
located .
(2 ) The legislative body of any political subdivision adopting airport zoning
regulations under KRS 183.750 to 183.758 may delegate the duty of administering
and enforcing such regulations to any administrative agency under its jurisdiction or
may create a new administrative agency to perform such duty , but such adminis-
trative agency shall not be or include any member of the board of appeals . The
duties of such administrative agency shall include that of hearing and deciding all
permits under subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 183.753 , but such agency shall not have or
exercise any of the powers delegated to the board of appeals . ( 1946 , c . 49 , § 5 ( 1,2 ) )
29

183.755 Board of appeals . ( 1) Airport zoning regulations adopted under KRS
183.750 to 183.758 shall provide for a board of appeals to have and exercise the fol-
lowing powers :
( a) To hear and decide appeals from an order , requirement , decision , or de-
termination made by the administrative agency in the enforcement of KRS 183.750
to 183.758 or of any ordinance adopted pursuant thereto ;
( b ) To hear and decide special exceptions to the terms of the ordinance upon
which such board may be required to pass under such ordinance ;
( c ) To hear and decide specific variances under subsection ( 3 ) of KRS 183.753 .
( 2 ) Where a zoning board of appeals or adjustment already exists , it may be
appointed as the board of appeals . Otherwise , the board of appeals shall consist of
five members , each to be appointed for a term of three years and to be removable
for cause by the appointing authority upon written charges and after public hearing .
( 3 ) The board shall adopt rules in accordance with the provisions of any
ordinance adopted under KRS 183.750 to 183.758 . Meetings of the board shall be
held at the call of the chairman and at such other times as the board may determine .
The chairman , or in his absence the acting chairman , may aminister oaths and
compel the attendance of witnesses . All meetings of the board shall be public . The
board shall keep minutes of its proceedings , showing the vote of each member upon
each question , or , if absent or failing to vote , indicating such fact , and shall keep
records of its examinations and other official actions , all of which shall immediately
be filed in the office of the board and shall be a public record .
(4 ) Appeals to the board may be taken by any person aggrieved , or by any
officer , department , board , or bureau of the political subdivision affected , by any
decision of the administrative agency . An appeal must be taken within a reasonable
time , as provided by the rules of the board , by filing with the agency from which
the appeal is taken , and with the board , a notice of appeal specifying the grounds
thereof . The agency from which the appeal is taken shall forthwith transmit to the
board all the papers constituting the record upon which the action appealed from
was taken .
( 5 ) An appeal shall stay all proceedings in furtherance of the action appealed
from , unless the agency from which the appeal is taken certifies to the board , after
the notice of appeal has been filed with it , that by reason of the facts stated in the
certificate , a stay would , in its opinion , cause imminent peril to life or property . In
such case proceedings shall not be stayed otherwise than by a restraining order
which may be granted by the board or by a court of record on application of notice
to the agency from which the appeal is taken and on due cause shown .
( 6 ) The board shall fix reasonable time for the hearing of the appeal , give
public notice and due notice to the parties in interest , and decide the same within a
reasonable time . Upon the hearing any party may appear in person or by agent or
by attorney .
( 7 ) The board may , in conformity with the provisions of KRS 183.750 to
183.758 , reverse or affirm , wholly or partly , or modify the order , requirement ,
decision , or determination appealed from and may make such order , requirement ,
decision , or determination as ought to be made , and to that end shall have all the
powers of the administrative agency from which the appeal is taken .
( 8 ) The concurring vote of a majority of the members of the board shall be
sufficient to reverse any order , requirement , decision , or determination of the
administrative agency , or to decide in favor of the applicant on any matter upon
which it is required to pass under any such ordinance , or to effect any variation in
such ordinance . ( 1946 , c . 49 , § 5 ) ( 3 ) )
30

183.756 Appeal from board of appeals to courts . ( 1 ) Any person aggrieved by
any decision of the board of appeals , or any taxpayer , or any officer , department ,
board , or bureau of the political subdivision , may present to the circuit court a
verified petition setting forth that the decision is illegal , in whole or in part , and
specifying the grounds of illegality . Such petition shall be presented to the court
within thirty days after the decision is filed in the office of the board .
(2 ) Upon presentation of such petition the court may allow a writ of certiorari
directed to the board of appeals to review such decision of the board . The allow-
ance of the writ shall not stay proceedings upon the decision appealed from , but the
court may , on application , on notice to the board and on due cause shown , grant
a restraining order .
( 3 ) The board of appeals shall not be required to return the original papers
acted upon by it , but it shall be sufficient to return certified or sworn copies
thereof or of such portions thereof as may be called for by the writ . The return
shall concisely set forth such other facts as may be pertinent and material to show
the grounds of the decision appealed from and shall be verified .
(4 ) The court shall have exclusive jurisdiction to affirm , modify or set aside
the decision brought up for review , in whole or in part , and , if need be , to order
further proceedings by the board of appeals . The findings of fact by the board , if
supported by substantial evidence , shall be accepted by the court as conclusive ,
and no objection to a decision of the board shall be considered by the court unless
such objection shall have been urged before the board , or if it was not so urged,
unless there were reasonable grounds for failure to do so .
( 5 ) Costs shall not be allowed against the board of appeals unless it appears
to the court that it acted with gross negligence, in bad faith , or with malice , in
making the decision appealed from . ( 1946 , c. 49 , § 6 )
183.757 Acquisition of air right , easement or property for airport protection .
In any case in which it is desired to remove , lower , or otherwise terminate a non-
conforming use ; or the approach protection necessary cannot , because of noncon-
stitutional limitations , be provided by airport zoning regulations under KRS 183.750
to 183.758 ; or it appears advisable that the necessary approach protection be pro-
vided by acquisition of property rights rather than by airport zoning regulations ,
the political subdivision within which the property or nonconforming use is located
or the political subdivision owning the airport or served by it may acquire , by pur-
chase , grant , or condemnation in the manner provided by KRS 416.120 , such an air
right , easement , or other estate or interest in the property or nonconforming use in
question as may be necessary to effectuate the purposes of KRS 183.750 to 183.758 .
(1946 , c . 49 , § 8 )
183.758 Injunction against violation of zoning regulations . In addition to the
penalty prescribed by subsection ( 4 ) of KRS 183.990 , the political subdivision or
agency adopting zoning regulations under KRS 183.750 to 183.757 may institute , in
any court of competent jurisdiction , an action to prevent , restrain , correct or abate
any violation of KRS 183.750 to 183.757 , or of airport zoning regulations adopted
thereunder , or of any order or ruling made in connection with their administration
or enforcement , and the court shall adjudge to the plaintiff such relief , by way of
injunction ( which may be mandatory ) or otherwise , as may be proper under all the
facts and circumstances of the case , in order fully to effectuate the purposes of
KRS 183.750 to 183.757 and of the regulations adopted and orders and rulings made
pursuant thereto . ( 1946 , c . 49 , § 7 )
183.990 ( 165-56 ) Penalties . ( 1) Any person who violates any of the provi-
sions of KRS 183.020 to 183.130 with respect to operation of aircraft shall be fined
not more than one hundred dollars or imprisoned not more than ninety days , or

KRS 276.450 ( 1 ) No railroad company , street railway company , steamboat
company or other person engaged in the transportation of passengers within this
state shall knowingly transport within this state , or into this state for sale , storage
or use therein , in any vehicle containing passengers , or in any vehicle attached to

not plainly and legibly marked with the name of the compound and the words

paper published in each of the cities of Louisville , Lexington , Paducah and Coving-

section , or send or receive any explosive compound that is not packed as required
by such rules . No conductor of a train , captain of a steamboat , or person in charge of
any public vehicle engaged in carrying passengers or freight shall fail to observe
such rules in transporting an explosive compound .

do so .
( 3 ) The company shall keep a record of the articles sold and prices obtained
therefor , and shall pay any surplus remaining after payment of charges to the owner
of the articles , if demanded by him within two years from the date of sale .

pany within this state receiving property for transportation between points in this

(4 ) Execution .
Every application shall be signed by or on behalf of the person filing the same
and shall be verified by the person signing the same in the manner required by
Subsection ( a ) hereof . Any general partner may sign on behalf of a partnership .
Documents filed by a corporation , business trust , or other similar organizations must
be signed by an officer who is duly authorized to take such action . Each person
filing any such document shall furnish such additional copies and shall make such
service of the document on other persons as the Commission may at any time
require . Such documents shall be delivered in person , through the mails or other-
wise to the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission in Frankfort , Kentucky , and shall
be deemed to have been filed on the date on which they are actually received .
( a ) Verification .
Every verification shall set forth that the person verifying the document has
read and is familiar with the contents thereof and the attached exhibits , if any ;
that he intends and desires that in granting or denying the relief requested , the
Commission shall place full and complete reliance upon the accuracy of each and
every statement therein contained ; that he is familiar with the facts therein set
forth; that to the best of his information and belief , every statement contained in
the instrument is true and no such statement is misleading .
( b ) Retention of Papers by the Commission .
When any formal application , complaint or petition is denied , dismissed or
permitted to be withdrawn , in whole or in part , the said application , complaint or
petition , and all documents filed with the Commission pertaining thereto shall be
retained in the files of the Commission . When any proceeding instituted by the
Commission is dismissed, terminated or rescinded , all documents filed with the
Commission pertaining thereto shall be retained in the files of the Commission ;
Provided , that this paragraph shall not apply to documents filed with the Com-
mission in any proceeding on the basis of a stipulation that such documents will
be returned to the parties so filing , when the purpose for which the documents
are filed has been served .
( 5 ) Notice of Proceedings .
Upon proper filing of an application for certificate of public convenience and
necessity to operate a common carrier or charter operator in intrastate commerce ,
the Commission shall fix a time and place for a hearing thereon . Notice of such
filing and of the hearing shall be executed for the Commission by its Secretary or
Director and shall be mailed not less than thirty days , exclusive of the date of
mailing , before such hearing , addressed to all holders of certificates and applicants
for such certificates , mayors in the Commonwealth of Kentucky along the routes
and any further person or persons deemed by the Commission to have a substantial
interest in the subject matter of any proceeding .
( 6 ) Appearances by Third Persons and Formal Interventions .
( a ) Any person, including the Federal and State Governments political sub-
division thereof , Federal or State aviation commission or other public body , may
appear at any public hearing and may present any evidence which is relevent to
the issue . Such persons may also suggest questions or interrogatories to be pro-
pounded by Public Counsel to witnesses called by other persons . With the consent
of the presiding officer or of the Commission , if the hearing is held before the
Commission , such persons may also cross - examine witnesses directly .
(b ) Formal Interventions .
Any person having a substantial interest in the subject matter of any pro-
ceeding may petition for leave to intervene in such proceeding and may become
39

a party thereto upon compliance with the provisions of this paragraph . In general ,
such petitions will not be granted unless
(I) That such person has a statutory right to be made a party to such pro-
ceeding; or ,
(II ) That such person will or may be bound by the order to be entered in
the proceeding ; or ,
(III ) That such person has a property or financial interest which may not
be adequately represented by existing parties , if such intervention would not unduly
broaden the issue or delay the proceeding . However , the denial of such petition
for leave to intervene shall not prevent the petitioner from participating in the pro-
ceeding in the manner prescribed in paragraph ( a ) of this section .
Unless otherwise ordered by the Commission , every petition for leave to inter-
vene shall be filed with the Commission not later than ten days prior to the hear-
ing , or in the event that a pre - hearing conference is to be held such petition shall
be filed prior to the first such conference . Copies of the petition shall be mailed
or delivered to each party to the proceeding prior to the filing of the petition . The
Commission , however , may pass upon any such petition without receiving testi-
mony or argument either from the petitioner or from other parties to the proceed-
ing . The petition shall clearly set forth the interest of the petitioner , and shall
otherwise comply with the requirements of the Regulations .
No petition for leave to intervene , not filed within the time limited by the pre-
ceding paragraph of this subsection , will be entertained by the Commission unless
the petitioner shall clearly show good cause for his failure to file such petition
within the time so limited .
(7) Procedure Before Hearing Officer .
Any hearing other than a final determination hearing held by the authority
of the Commission , shall be presided over by one or more members of the Com-
mission as Hearing Officer or Officers , as set out by section ( a) hereof .
The functions of the Hearing Officer , as well as those presiding in decisions
shall be conducted in an impartial manner in accord with the requirements of
Section 183.570 . 183.590 and 183.610 , Kentucky Revised Statutes , with due regard
for the rights of all parties to the record , as well as the facts and the law, and
consistent with the orderly and prompt dispatch of hearing and determination .
Such Hearing Officer or Officers , except to the extent required for the dis-
position of Ex Parte matters authorized by law , shall not :
( 1) Engage in interviews with , or receive evidence or arguments from , any
party to the record , directly or indirectly , except upon opportunity for all other
parties to be present and in accord with the public procedure authorized by these
regulations and laws of this Commonwealth .
(2) Grant , authorize , approve , order , dismiss , deny or refuse the application
for a certificate of convenience and necessity , nor suspend , revoke , set aside ,
rescind , amend or terminate any certificate already issued ; but shall hear evi-
dence, receive exhibits , motions , pleas , briefs and any and all other matters in
connection with the substantiation of the issuance of a certificate of convenience
and necessity , and shall present the complete record of the hearing so held on the
application for a certificate or hearing for revocation , to the Commission , who , in
Joint session , shall review all the record offered , and the Commission shall , after
due consideration of the complete record , dispose of the matter under consideration .
Upon the filing of a timely affidavit of personal bias or disqualification , or
conduct contrary to law of any such Hearing Officer at any stage of the proceed
40

ings , the Commission , after hearing the facts , shall make findings , conclusions , and
determinations as to such disqualification , which shall become a part of the record
in the case , and be reviewable .
( a ) Whereas , it is more convenient and expedient for less than all the mem-
bers of the Commission to conduct the evidence taking hearing , as hereinabove
mentioned , it is decided by the commission that the Chairman of the Kentucky
Aeronautics Commission , or the member most conveniently situated , by direction
of the Chairman , to act as a Hearing Officer in holding and conducting said hear-
ing in accordance with the rules and regulations of the Commission and the laws
of this Commonwealth .
(b ) Exhibits .
Wherever practicable , two copies of each exhibit ( in addition to the original
offered in evidence at the hearing ) shall be furnished for the use of the Hearing
Officer , and one copy should be furnished to public counsel . One of such copies
will be made available for inspection for all persons present at the hearing .
Excerpts from lengthy documents or portions of the record in other proceedings
before the Commission should be offered in the form of exhibits and copies
furnished as above provided . Such exhibits may be received in evidence subject
to objection and rebuttal by public counsel or other counsel after opportunity to
examine the exhibits in question and the source from which the same was taken .
( c ) The Commission shall , at all times , be free to evaluate any evidence pro-
duced before it and to draw its own reasonable conclusions as to the truth and
probative force of such evidence .
Every party before such Hearing Officer shall have the right to be represented
by counsel , cross examination and submission of rebuttal evidence in open hear-
ings . The Commission shall receive and consider evidence with respect to other
forms of transportation or service , or facilities thereof , in determining whether
public convenience and necessity require an applicant's proposed service and opera-
tion . The Commission shall have the right to be represented by public counsel ,
who shall have the right to examine and cross examine witnesses and submit
evidence . The principles of relevancy , materiality , probative force and substan-
tiality as recognized in judicial proceedings of equitable nature , shall govern the
proof , decision and administrative review of all conclusions of fact.
( 8 ) Determination by Commission ; Orders of Commission ; Rehearing .
( a ) Determination of any proceeding shall be made by the Commission , includ-
ing any necessary order or orders in connection therewith . Orders of the Com-
mission shall be served upon all parties to said proceeding by the Commission and
shall become effective thirty days after such service .
( b ) Any of said parties to any such hearing may upon proper petition be
granted a rehearing . If any rehearing is granted within thirty days after the
issuance of any order then such order shall not become effective until thirty days
after the determination of the Commission following such rehearing proceedings .
Any party may petition for rehearing , reargument or reconsideration of any final
order by the Commission in a proceeding , or for further hearing before decision
by the Commission . The matters of record claimed to have been erroneously
decided must be specified and the alleged errors and the grounds relied upon must
be briefly and specifically stated in the petition .
If a final order of the Commission is sought to be vacated or modified by
reason of matters which have arisen since the hearing , or of a consequence which
would result from a compliance therewith , or both , the new matter , the resulting
consequence , or both , which are relied upon by the petitioner must each be set
41

forth in the petition . Where the petition is based wholly or in part upon new
matter , the petition must contain a statement that the petitioner , with due diligence ,
could not have known or discovered the new matter prior to the time of the hear-
ing .
The petition for rehearing must set forth a brief statement of the relief sought
by the petitioner , and be filed in octuplet .
Such petition for rehearing , reargument , or reconsideration , must be filed
within thirty days after service of the order sought to be vacated or modified . After
the expiration of said thirty days , such a petition may be filed only by leave of the
Commission granted pursuant to formal application upon a showing of reasonable
grounds for failure to file the petition within the prescribed thirty -day period .
Any such petition or application shall be served by the petitioner or applicant upon
all parties to the proceeding or their attorneys of record .
( c ) Oral Arguments ; Proposed Findings ; Briefs .
Upon request of any party , the Commission may permit oral argument before
final determination of the case . Oral arguments may be transcribed but shall not
constitute a part of the record .
If a case presents unusually difficult questions of fact or law, the Commission
may permit or require the submission of proposed findings of fact or conclusions
of law , or of written briefs to aid in the preparation of a decision .
(d) Report and Decision .
In each case a report and decision will ordinarily be made and the report
will state that :
( I ) Names of persons who will receive copies of the same ;
( II ) Time within which exceptions will be filed and exchanged ;
(III ) Time thereafter within which briefs relating to such exceptions are to
be filed and exchanged , and may give other instructions relating to procedure after
hearing .
After a date has been set for the submission of proposed findings of fact or
conclusions of law , written briefs , or the filing of exceptions to presiding officer's
or officers ' report , or briefs thereon , such date may be postponed upon the proper
cause shown , but any such postponement shall not be granted less than three
days prior to the date originally set for the filing thereof , except in cases involving
unusual circumstances imposing substantial hardship upon the requesting party
or parties .
(9 ) Memoranda in Opposition or Support .
Each protest or memorandum of opposition to or in support of the issuance ,
alteration , amendment , modification , suspension , revocation or abandonment of
certificate of public convenience and necessity shall conform to the requirements
with regard to the filing of applications and hearings thereon as provided by section
4 hereof . Each such protest or memorandum shall clearly state on its face the
particular proceeding in which it is desired to be filed and shall contain a concise
but clear statement of the grounds of opposition or support . At the time of filing
any such protest or memorandum with the Commission , the person filing it shall
serve a copy thereof upon each party to the particular proceeding and upon such
other persons as the Commission may require . No such protest or memorandum
will be received as or be considered to constitute evidence in the particular pro-
ceeding of any fact mentioned or discussed therein . However , evidence in support
of any such protest or memorandum may be presented by or on behalf of the person
filing it in the manner provided in Sections 7 and 8 hereof . Parties may file relevant
or responsive pleadings timely but are not required to do so . The secretary of the

and addresses of the parties to the proceeding .
( 10 ) Revocation and Abandonment .
Any application provided for in Section 183.610 , Kentucky Revised Statutes
1944 , shall be filed and proceedings conducted thereon in the same manner as
provided in regard to other applications and also in accordance with these
Regulations .
(11) Record .
A transcript of testimony adduced and exhibits admitted in conformity with
Section 183.620 , Kentucky Revised Statutes 1944 , together with all pleadings , excep-
tions , motions , requests and papers filed by the parties other than separately pre-
sented briefs or arguments of law , shall constitute the complete and exclusive record
and be made available for the inspection of all parties .
Any person desiring to receive a copy of the record of the proceedings may
have same by paying the cost of the preparation of the copy of the record .
KAC- 2. It is ordered that before any certificate of convenience and necessity
for the operation of aircraft operating as a common carrier and / or charter operator
flying in intra - state commerce is granted by the Commission , the applicant shall file
with the Commission a policy of insurance , payable to the Commonwealth , binding
the obligor to pay final judgment rendered against him , arising out of the death or
injury to any passenger , or loss of or damage to property while in transit , or death
of or injury to other persons , or damages to their property , or any act or omission
connected with the operation of aircraft by the applicant .
The insurance policy shall be in the minimum sums of five thousand ( $ 5000.00 )
dollars per passenger , ten thousand ( $ 10,000.00 ) dollars per collision or accident
for each aircraft operated , for death of or injury to persons , and in the further sum
of one thousand ( $ 1000.00 ) dollars for each aircraft operating for damages to
property .
КАС- 3. 1.00 Certificate Required . No person shall operate or permit the
operation of any aircraft in scheduled intrastate common carrier air transportation
carrying mail , goods , or persons , or any combination thereof , unless such person is
possessed of a valid certificate of convenience and necessity issued by the Kentucky
Aeronautics Commission .
1.01 Service Performed . No person shall operate or hold himself out to
operate , perform or render in service as related to the carriage of mail , goods , or
persons in contact or instrument operation until rated competent to render such
service as authorized by the certificate of convenience and necessity issued by the
Kentucky Aeronautics Commission . This shall not apply , however , to any person
in operation on June 13 , 1944 , to whom has been issued a certificate of convenience
and necessity by the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission . Such person shall , how-
ever , comply with all regulations of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission govern-
ing intrastate air operations after June 1, 1945 .
1.10 Operation Schedules . Operation schedules shall be set up by the air
carrier with due regard to sufficient time for the adequate servicing with fuel and
oil at intermediate stops on the basis of a cruising speed of the aircraft not to
exceed the specific power output of the engine or engines as operated in the air-
craft . Block - to - block time shall be used in establishing time from stop to stop .
1.20 Route Operation . No person shall be issued a certificate of public con-
venience and necessity to operate aircraft in scheduled intrastate common carrier
43

air transportation over any route or part thereof in Kentucky until rated competent
to operate thereover in its air carrying operating certificate .
1.200 Off - route Operation . No person granted a certificate by the Kentucky
Aeronautics Commission shall operate aircraft other than on the routes provided
by the certificate except that this provision does not exclude operations in emer-
gency to any point necessary for safety requirements , nor does it exclude charter
operations conducted separately by the air carrier .
1.21 Regular Route . The conduct of operations by scheduled intrastate com-
mon carrier air transportation shall at all times be in strict accordance with the
terms of its certificate of convenience and necessity issued by the Kentucky Aero-
nautics Commission .
1.22 Regular Stops . Regular terminals and intermediate stops shall be used
only as specified in the certificate of convenience and necessity .
1.23 Alternate Airports . Regular terminals , intermediate stops , or other ade-
quate airports may be used as alternates when used for the purpose of complying
with clearance requirements .
1.30 Aircraft Operation . No scheduled intrastate air carrier shall operate any
aircraft until it is rated competent with respect thereto .
1.31 Single - Engine Aircraft . No single - engine aircraft shall be operated on
a scheduled intrastate air route in the Commonwealth of Kentucky under night
instrument conditions with passengers .
1.32 Instruments . No aircraft shall be operated in Kentucky in scheduled air
transportation service unless such aircraft is possessed of all instruments required
by the provisions of Section 1.72 , and 1.73 , of these regulations , whichever is ap-
plicable .
1.33 Equipment . No aircraft shall be operated in scheduled air transportation
in the Commonwealth of Kentucky unless such aircraft is possessed of all equip-
ment required by the provisions of 1.72 , and 1.73 of this regulation , whichever is
applicable .
1.331 Seat Belts . All aircraft operated in intrastate scheduled air transporta-
tion within the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall maintain fastened safety belts
at all times .
1.35 Maintenance .
1.3500 General . All aircraft operating in intrastate scheduled air carrier
operations in Kentucky shall be maintained in continuous condition of airworthiness
as provided by the Civil Air Regulations of the Civil Air Administration in accord-
ance with accepted standards and practices .
1.3501 Organization . No person shall operate or permit to be operated any
aircraft in intrastate air transportation under a certificate of convenience and
necessity issued by the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission until it shall have set
up and have in operation a maintenance organization , which organization shall be
responsible for the continuous airworthiness of all aircraft , engines , propellers ,
accessories and instruments for the proper maintenance of adequate facilities , for
the adequacy and competency of maintenance personnel , and for the preparation
and dissemination of such maintenance reports as are required by the Kentucky
Aeronautics Commission . On and after June 1, 1945 , it shall be required of all
persons operating aircraft in scheduled intrastate transportation under a certifi-
cate issued by the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission that the maintenance of all
aircraft owned by it shall be performed in a shop owned by the air carrier which
44

shop has a repair station rating issued by the Civil Aeronautics Administration for
the repair of aircraft used by the carrier . Under no condition shall the repair
station possess less than ratings in ( 1 ) Aircraft of Composite Construction and
( 2 ) Aircraft Engines .
1.3514 Refueling Requirements . The following rules will govern the opera-
tions incident to the refueling of air carrier aircraft in Kentucky :
( 1 ) Water elimination facilities shall be provided at all refueling points .
( 2 ) Daily check for the presence of water in aircraft tanks shall be made .
( 3 ) During refueling the aircraft and fueling dispensing apparatus shall both
be grounded to a point of zero electrical potential .
(4 ) No smoking and no fires or flames shall be permitted in the immediate
vicinity of an aircraft while refueling is being accomplished .
( 5 ) When passengers are permitted to remain in the cabin while refueling , it
shall be the pilot's duty to enforce the no smoking prohibition .
1.3515 Alteration and Repairs . Air carrier aircraft , engines , props , and ap-
proved components shall be altered or repaired only in conformity to the procedures
and methods approved by the Civil Aeronautics Administration .
1.3516 Records . Current records shall be kept of the total time of service , the
time since last overhaul , and time since last inspection on all aircraft , engines , props
and components . Current records shall be kept of all aircraft , engine or component
failures and a report shall be submitted to the Commission every three months
period , beginning with January 1 of each year .
1.352 Failure Report . It shall be the duty of the Chief Mechanic or Chief of
Operation to submit to the Commission a detailed report of every failure of air-
craft , engine , radio or component while in flight on scheduled intrastate operations
within 48 hours of occurrence .
1.4 Maintenance Manual . In order properly to maintain flight equipment
each intrastate common carrier shall prepare and file with the Commission a main-
tenance manual for the use and guidance of the maintenance personnel . The
manual shall outline instructions covering the overhaul , check , inspection , servicing
of flight equipment , daily inspection and check routine ; and shall contain as a
part , all bulletins issued by the Civil Aeronautics Administration or manufacturer
which pertains to the aircraft , engines and components in use by the carrier .
1.5 Approval . No intrastate air carrier shall utilize any pilot until said pilot
has been approved by the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission .
1.51 Airmen . No pilot shall be entitled to a certificate of approval to operate
aircraft in scheduled intrastate common carrier operations unless he meets the
following minimum requirements :
( a ) A valid commercial certificate issued by the Civil Aeronautics Administra-
tion with proper horsepower rating to fly carrier's aircraft .
(b ) A valid instrument rating issued by the Civil Aeronautics Administration .
( c ) A minimum of 500 hours of visual contact day and 50 hours of actual instru-
ment flight .
(d) Familiarization of not less than 25 hours over routes flown .
1.52 Co -Pilot . No co -pilot shall be required in single - engine operation during
daylight hours by intrastate air carrier when passengers are carried .
1.53 Manifests . It shall be the duty of the pilot accurately to fill out before
each flight two copies of Form KAC — 1, to place one copy in the station file , and to
45

return the duplicate to the Operational Manager . Form KAC- 1 shall be evidence
of having complied with weight and balance limitations of the aircraft and shall
contain such information and be in such form as prescribed by the Commission .

be operated any aircraft unless at the time of the operation the aircraft possesses a
valid airworthiness certificate .
1.71 Weather Minimums . All intrastate air carriers shall comply with pre-
scribed weather minimums of Civil Air Regulation Section 61 , Parts 61.7107 to
61.71091 inclusive , except Part 61.71082 С.

visual - contact day operation unless each aircraft meets the following specifications :

2. Have a minimum of four ( 4 ) seats available for passengers or have a pay

on day instrument or Over - the - Top flight unless said aircraft has installed and
properly operating :

2. Fuel Indicator

Operations Manual . No intrastate air carrier shall operate aircraft unless
each aircraft is provided with an Operations Manual which shall contain the fol-
lowing information :
1. Detailed description of route , together with map and all other pertinent
information .
2. Instrument procedures at terminals or designated alternates .
3. Operational data of aircraft .
4. Weather minimums at the terminals , or designated alternates .
5. Emergency procedure .
1.741 Approval . No intrastate air carrier shall operate aircraft without having
its operation manual approved by the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission .
1.75 Airmen . No intrastate air carrier shall utilize any pilot until he is
thoroughly familiar with the contents of carrier operational manual .
1.8 Applications for certificate of approval or rating , as provided by Sections
1.01 , 1.20 , 1.30 , 1.3501 , 1.5 , 1.7 and 1.741 hereof , shall be made in writing to the Di-
rector , and accompanied by such evidences of compliance or operation as deemed
necessary by the applicant . The Director shall submit all such applications to the
Commission at their first regular meeting after receipt thereof .
1.99 No paragraph or part thereof herein contained shall be so construed as
to void or violate any rule or regulation enacted by the Civil Aeronautics Admin-
istration .

DEFINITIONS
1. As used in this regulation , unless the context otherwise requires , " pilot"
means any individual who engages as the person in command in the navigation of
aircraft .
2. " Person " shall mean and include any individual , co -partnership , association ,
corporation , or other form of organization , and their lessee , trustee , or receiver,
appointed by the court .
3. "Air carrier " means any citizen of the United States who undertakes ,
whether directly or indirectly or by lease , or by any other arrangement , to engage
in intrastate air transportation .

The policy to which this endorsement is attached is written under and pursuant
to the provisions of the Kentucky Aeronautics Acts of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and is payable to the Commonwealth of Kentucky for
the benefit of all persons who may suffer personal injuries or property damage
due to any negligence of the assured , his or its agents , servants or employees in the
operation or use of any aircraft operating under the assured's certificate , permit or
license issued by the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission , except employees of the
assured who may suffer injuries in the course of their employment .
In consideration of the premium stipulated in the policy to which this endorse-
ment is attached , the insurer agrees to pay any final judgment which may be
rendered against the insured holding such certificate , permit or license for per-
sonal injury to any person , and for the damage to any property due to the negli-
gence of the insured , his or its agents , servants or employees in the operation or
use of any aircraft listed in the original application of the insured or any additional ,
substituted or emergency aircraft operated under such certificate , permit or li-
cense of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission within the limits of the schedule
contained in this endorsement ; except , however , for the personal injury to any
employee of the insured which may have been suffered in the course of his employ-
ment , or for the damage to any property owned by , leased , hired or in the care ,
custody or control of the insured except the property of passengers or small pack-
ages carried in or upon passenger - carrying aircraft .
The insurer further agrees that upon its failure to pay such final judgment
within sixty ( 60 ) days from the date of its rendition or final rendition on appeal , the
judgment creditor may maintain an action against it in any court of competent
jurisdiction to enforce such payment . This policy shall constitute a continuing in-
demnity , the amount of which shall not be reduced as to any succeeding claim by
any payment of any claim or any judgment or by any previous accident .
No condition , provision , stipulation or limitation contained in the policy or any
other endorsement thereon , nor the violation of the same by the insured shall
affect in any way the right of the person injured in his person or property by the
negligence of the insured or relieve the company from the liability provided for
in this endorsement , or from the payment to any such person of any such judg-
ment , to the extent and in the amounts set forth in the schedule shown hereon ,
except employees of the insured engaged in the course of their employment ; but
the conditions , provisions , stipulations and limitations contained in the policy and

КАС- 11 . Pursuant to the authority vested in the Kentucky Aeronautics Com-
mission by Section 183.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , the following regula-
tions pertaining to air traffic control are hereby adopted :
( 1 ) Authority of pilot . The pilot in command of an aircraft shall be directly
responsible for its safe operation .
( 2 ) Careless or reckless operation . No person shall operate an aircraft in a
careless or reckless manner so as to endanger the life or property of
another
( 3 ) Right - of -way and proximity
( a ) Distress . An aircraft in distress has the right - of - way over all other
traffic .
( b ) Order . Aircraft in flight , except in emergencies , shall have right - of-
way in the following order :
( 1 ) balloons , fixed or free ( an airship not under control is classed as a
free balloon )
(2 ) gliders ,
( 3 ) airships ,
( 4 ) airplanes towing gliders ,
( 5 ) airplanes and rotorplanes .
( c ) Right - of - way of similar type aircraft .
( 1 ) Converging . When two aircraft are on crossing courses at ap-
proximately the same altitude , the aircraft on the left shall give
way.
( 2 ) Approaching head on . When two aircraft are approaching head on ,
or approximately so, and there is danger of collision , each shall
alter its course to the right .
( 3 ) Overtaking . An overtaken aircraft has the right - of - way and the
overtaking aircraft , whether climbing , descending , or in level
flight , shall alter its course to the right .
( 4 ) Landing . Aircraft while landing or maneuvering for a landing
have the right - of - way over other aircraft in flight or on the surface .
( d ) Proximity . Aircraft shall be flown at least 500 feet apart except by
prearrangement of the pilots in command of the aircraft .
( 4 ) Aerobatic Flight. An aircraft shall not be aerobatically flown .
( a ) so as to endanger air traffic , or
( b ) over the congested areas of cities, towns , settlements , or open - air as-
semblies of persons ,
( c ) except where permission has previously been granted by the Kentucky
Aeronautics Commission .
( 5 ) Minimum safe altitudes . Except when necessary for taking off and landing ,
aircraft shall be flown :
55

( a ) when over the congested areas of cities , towns , settlements , or open-
air assemblies of persons , at altitudes sufficient to permit emergency
landings outside such areas and in no case less than 1,000 feet above
such areas , and
(b ) when elsewhere than as specified in paragraph ( a ) , at an altitude of not
less than 500 feet , except over water or areas where flying at a lower
altitude will not involve hazard to persons or property on the surface .
( 6 ) Operation on and in the vicinity of airports . Aircraft shall be operated on
and in the vicinity of airports in accordance with the following rules :
( a ) Prior to and during taxiing , taking off , and landing , a pilot shall :
( 1) observe other traffic and take precaution to avoid collision .
( 2 ) conform to the flow of traffic , and
( 3 ) if a control tower is in operation , maintain contact with such tower ,
either visually or by radio , to receive any traffic control instruc-
tions which may be issued .
(b ) After taking off or when approaching for landing , all turns shall be
made to the left unless a different procedure has been authorized by
the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission , or unless otherwise instructed
by a control tower .
( c) If a landing is not intended , aircraft shall be flown so as to avoid or
conform to the traffic pattern formed by aircraft landing and taking off .
( d ) The Kentucky Aeronautics Commission shall coordinate the flight activ-
ities of any airport with the flight activities of any other airport lo-
cated within 5 miles in order to avoid conflict in air traffic resulting
from the proximity of such airports .
( 7 ) Aircraft lights . During the hours of darkness :
( a ) All aircraft in flight shall display position lights
( b ) All aircraft parked or moved within or in dangerous proximity to the
useable portion of any airport used for , or available to , night flight
operations shall be clearly illuminated or lighted , unless the parking
area is marked with obstruction lights .
( c ) All aircraft on the water and not under way or which are moored in
navigational lanes shall display a white anchor light or position lights .
( 8 ) All airports shall display an international circle as a marker in a place con-
spicuous from flight , together with an arrow or other indicator pointed in
the direction of true north , and display the name of the particular airport ,
together with sufficient markings to make visible from flight the runways ,
useable and in use , at said airports .
( 9 ) All airports shall display and maintain in operation wind socks , wind
" T's " or other approved wind indicators so that same are visible from
flight .
КАС- 12 . Pursuant to the authority vested in the Kentucky Aeronautics Com-
mission by Section 183.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , the following regula-
tions are hereby adopted :
1. All airports operating within the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall at all
times have available in a conspicuous , readily accessible place , fire extinguishers
in sufficient number and size to control all reasonable and probable fires .
2. All airports shall maintain and keep in an operating condition telephone
facilities .
